---
title: "Data Access"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## 2024 ACS

Q

```{r}
library(tidycensus)
library(tidyverse)

# Pull ACS 5-year estimates for Cuyahoga County census tracts
cuyahoga_acs <- get_acs(
  geography = "tract",
  state = "OH",
  county = "Cuyahoga",
  year = 2024,  
  survey = "acs5",
  variables = c(
    total_pop           = "B01003_001",
    median_hh_income    = "B19013_001",
    poverty_count       = "B17001_002",
    bachelors           = "B15003_022",
    masters             = "B15003_023",
    professional        = "B15003_024",
    doctorate           = "B15003_025",
    owner_occupied      = "B25003_002",
    median_gross_rent   = "B25064_001",
    white_alone         = "B02001_002",
    total_race          = "B02001_001"
  ),
  output = "wide"
) %>%
  select(-ends_with("M")) %>%
  rename_with(~ str_remove(.x, "E$"), ends_with("E")) %>%
  mutate(
    nonwhite_count       = total_race - white_alone,
    bachelors_up_count   = bachelors + masters + professional + doctorate
  ) %>%
  select(
    geoid              = GEOID,
    tract_name         = NAM,
    total_pop,
    median_hh_income,
    poverty_count,
    bachelors_up_count,
    owner_occupied,
    nonwhite_count,
    median_gross_rent
  )

# Quick check
glimpse(cuyahoga_acs)
summary(cuyahoga_acs)

# Save to CSV
write_csv(cuyahoga_acs, "cuyahoga_acs_2024.csv")
```

## Air Quality

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# Download EPA AQS annual summary for PM2.5, 2024
# This is a large national file (~50MB) so may take a moment
url <- "https://aqs.epa.gov/aqsweb/airdata/annual_conc_by_monitor_2024.zip"

temp <- tempfile()
download.file(url, temp)
aqs_raw <- read_csv(unz(temp, "annual_conc_by_monitor_2024.csv"))
unlink(temp)

# Filter to Cuyahoga County, Ohio
# FIPS: State = 39 (Ohio), County = 035 (Cuyahoga)
cuyahoga_aqs <- aqs_raw %>%
  filter(
    `State Name` == "Ohio",
    `County Name` == "Cuyahoga",
    (`Parameter Name` == "PM2.5 - Local Conditions" & 
       `Sample Duration` == "24-HR BLK AVG" &
       `Pollutant Standard` == "PM25 24-hour 2024") |
    (`Parameter Name` == "Ozone" & 
       `Sample Duration` == "8-HR RUN AVG BEGIN HOUR" &
       `Pollutant Standard` == "Ozone 8-hour 2015")
  ) %>%
  select(
    site_id          = `Site Num`,
    local_site_name  = `Local Site Name`,
    address          = Address,
    latitude         = Latitude,
    longitude        = Longitude,
    parameter        = `Parameter Name`,
    year             = Year,
    units            = `Units of Measure`,
    observation_pct  = `Observation Percent`,
    mean_value       = `Arithmetic Mean`,
    max_value        = `1st Max Value`,
    pct_98th         = `98th Percentile`,
    pct_75th         = `75th Percentile`,
    pct_50th         = `50th Percentile`
  )

cuyahoga_aqs %>% count(parameter)
glimpse(cuyahoga_aqs)
# Save
write_csv(cuyahoga_aqs, "cuyahoga_aqs_2024.csv")
```

## NOAA

Downloaded from the standard web tool <https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/>

![](images/clipboard-1153502819.png)

```{r}
library(rnoaa)
library(tidyverse)


weather_raw <- read_csv("4337957.csv")

selected_stations <- c(
  "USW00014820",  # Cleveland Hopkins International Airport (inland/SW, full data)
  "USW00004853",  # Cleveland Burke Lakefront Airport (lakefront, full data)
  "US1OHCY0024",  # Parma 1.9 NNW (south/suburban, precip only)
  "US1OHCY0048",  # Highland Hills 0.8 W (east, precip only)
  "US1OHCY0041"   # Lakewood 1.3 SSW (near lake/west, precip only)
)

cleveland_weather <- weather_raw %>%
  filter(STATION %in% selected_stations) %>%
  select(
    station_id    = STATION,
    station_name  = NAME,
    latitude      = LATITUDE,
    longitude     = LONGITUDE,
    date          = DATE,
    temp_max_c    = TMAX,
    temp_min_c    = TMIN,
    precip_mm     = PRCP,
    snowfall_mm   = SNOW,
    wind_speed_ms = AWND
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # NOAA stores temperature in tenths of degrees C
    temp_max_c    = temp_max_c / 10,
    temp_min_c    = temp_min_c / 10,
    # NOAA stores precipitation in tenths of mm
    precip_mm     = precip_mm  / 10,
    # NOAA stores wind speed in tenths of meters per second
    wind_speed_ms = wind_speed_ms / 10,
    # Snowfall is already in mm
    # Create a readable station type column
    station_type  = case_when(
      str_starts(station_id, "USW") ~ "Airport (NWS)",
      str_starts(station_id, "US1") ~ "Volunteer (CoCoRaHS)",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    station_id, station_name, station_type,
    latitude, longitude,
    date, temp_max_c, temp_min_c,
    precip_mm, snowfall_mm, wind_speed_ms
  ) %>%
  arrange(station_id, date)

# Quick check
glimpse(cleveland_weather)

cleveland_weather %>%
  group_by(station_id, station_name, station_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n_days       = n(),
    pct_tmax     = mean(!is.na(temp_max_c)),
    pct_prcp     = mean(!is.na(precip_mm)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  print()

write_csv(cleveland_weather, "cuyahoga_weather_2020_2024.csv")
```

## Building Permits

```{r}
building_permits <- read_csv("Building_Permits_-148757603738756233.csv")
glimpse(building_permits)


# Parse dates and check year distribution
building_permits <- building_permits %>%
  mutate(
    file_date  = mdy_hms(FILE_DATE),
    issue_date = mdy_hms(ISSUE_DATE),
    year       = year(file_date)
  )

# Year distribution
building_permits %>%
  count(year) %>%
  print(n = Inf)

# Permit type distribution
building_permits %>%
  count(PERMIT_TYPE, sort = TRUE) %>%
  print()

# Permit category distribution  
building_permits %>%
  count(PERMIT_CATEGORY, sort = TRUE) %>%
  print(n = 20)

# Neighborhood distribution
building_permits %>%
  count(DW_Neighborhood, sort = TRUE) %>%
  print(n = 20)


cleveland_permits_2024 <- building_permits %>%
  filter(
    year == 2024,
    PERMIT_TYPE == "Building Permit",
    PERMIT_CATEGORY == "New",
    CURRENT_TASK_STATUS == "Permit Closed"
  ) %>%
  select(
    permit_id        = PERMIT_ID,
    year,
    file_date,
    issue_date,
    permit_subtype   = PERMIT_SUBTYPE,
    job_description  = JOB_DESCRIPTION,
    job_value        = JOB_VALUE,
    total_fees_paid  = TOTAL_FEES_PAID,
    ward             = WARD,
    neighborhood     = DW_Neighborhood,
    latitude         = LAT,
    longitude        = LON
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    file_date  = as.Date(file_date),
    issue_date = as.Date(issue_date)
  )

glimpse(cleveland_permits_2024)
cleveland_permits_2024 %>% count(permit_subtype, sort = TRUE)
cleveland_permits_2024 %>% count(neighborhood, sort = TRUE) %>% print(n = Inf)
cleveland_permits_2024 %>% summary()

write_csv(cleveland_permits_2024, "cleveland_permits_2024.csv")

```

## BLS

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

bls_raw <- read_csv("BLS_2010-2026.csv", skip = 8)

cuyahoga_unemployment <- bls_raw %>%
  rename(
    year              = Year,
    month_name        = Period,
    labor_force       = `labor force`,
    employment        = employment,
    unemployment      = unemployment,
    unemployment_rate = `unemployment rate`
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Extract the flag before stripping it
    data_flag = case_when(
      str_detect(unemployment_rate, "\\(T\\)") ~ "revised",
      str_detect(unemployment_rate, "\\(P\\)") ~ "preliminary",
      str_detect(unemployment_rate, "\\(X\\)") ~ "unavailable",
      TRUE ~ "final"
    ),
    # Now strip flags and convert to numeric
    across(
      c(labor_force, employment, unemployment, unemployment_rate),
      ~ as.numeric(str_remove_all(.x, "\\(.*?\\)|-"))
    ),
    month = match(month_name, month.abb),
    date  = as.Date(paste(year, month, "01", sep = "-"))
  ) %>%
  filter(year >= 2010, year <= 2024) %>%
  select(
    date,
    year,
    month,
    month_name,
    labor_force,
    employment,
    unemployment,
    unemployment_rate,
    data_flag
  ) %>%
  arrange(date)

# Check flag distribution
cuyahoga_unemployment %>%
  count(data_flag)

cuyahoga_unemployment %>%
  filter(unemployment_rate > 10) %>%
  print()

glimpse(cuyahoga_unemployment)
write_csv(cuyahoga_unemployment, "cuyahoga_unemployment_2010_2024.csv")
```
